data.content:
Now I have a full picture. Here are all the **legacy patterns** found:

| # | Legacy Pattern | Modernization |
|---|----------------|---------------|
| 1 | `ToolsVersion` + XML namespace | Replaced by `Sdk` attribute |
| 2 | Manual `Import` of Common.props/CSharp.targets | Implicit via SDK |
| 3 | Default `Configuration`/`Platform` properties | SDK defaults |
| 4 | `ProjectGuid` | Not needed |
| 5 | `FileAlignment`, `Deterministic` | SDK defaults |
| 6 | `AutoGenerateBindingRedirects` | SDK default |
| 7 | Debug/Release PropertyGroups | All values are SDK defaults |
| 8 | Explicit `<Reference>` for System assemblies | Implicit for .NET Framework |
| 9 | Explicit `<Compile Include>` items | SDK implicit globbing |
| 10 | `Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs` | Auto-generated from project properties |
| 11 | `TargetFrameworkVersion v4.7.2` | `TargetFramework net472` |

Let me apply the modernization now.

arguments.old_str:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<Project ToolsVersion="15.0" xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/developer/msbuild/2003">
  <Import Project="$(MSBuildExtensionsPath)\$(MSBuildToolsVersion)\Microsoft.Common.props" Condition="Exists('$(MSBuildExtensionsPath)\$(MSBuildToolsVersion)\Microsoft.Common.props')" />
  <PropertyGroup>
    <Configuration Condition=" '$(Configuration)' == '' ">Debug</Configuration>
    <Platform Condition=" '$(Platform)' == '' ">AnyCPU</Platform>
    <ProjectGuid>{12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789012}</ProjectGuid>
    <OutputType>Library</OutputType>
    <RootNamespace>LegacyApp</RootNamespace>
    <AssemblyName>LegacyApp</AssemblyName>
    <TargetFrameworkVersion>v4.7.2</TargetFrameworkVersion>
    <FileAlignment>512</FileAlignment>
    <AutoGenerateBindingRedirects>true</AutoGenerateBindingRedirects>
    <Deterministic>true</Deterministic>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <PropertyGroup Condition=" '$(Configuration)|$(Platform)' == 'Debug|AnyCPU' ">
    <DebugSymbols>true</DebugSymbols>
    <DebugType>full</DebugType>
    <Optimize>false</Optimize>
    <OutputPath>bin\Debug\</OutputPath>
    <DefineConstants>DEBUG;TRACE</DefineConstants>
    <ErrorReport>prompt</ErrorReport>
    <WarningLevel>4</WarningLevel>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <PropertyGroup Condition=" '$(Configuration)|$(Platform)' == 'Release|AnyCPU' ">
    <DebugType>pdbonly</DebugType>
    <Optimize>true</Optimize>
    <OutputPath>bin\Release\</OutputPath>
    <DefineConstants>TRACE</DefineConstants>
    <ErrorReport>prompt</ErrorReport>
    <WarningLevel>4</WarningLevel>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <Reference Include="System" />
    <Reference Include="System.Core" />
    <Reference Include="System.Data" />
    <Reference Include="System.Xml" />
    <Reference Include="System.Xml.Linq" />
    <Reference Include="Microsoft.CSharp" />
  </ItemGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <Compile Include="Calculator.cs" />
    <Compile Include="StringHelper.cs" />
    <Compile Include="Properties\AssemblyInfo.cs" />
  </ItemGroup>
  <Import Project="$(MSBuildToolsPath)\Microsoft.CSharp.targets" />
</Project>
arguments.new_str:
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">

  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net472</TargetFramework>
    <RootNamespace>LegacyApp</RootNamespace>
    <AssemblyName>LegacyApp</AssemblyName>
    <Description>A legacy-style project for modernization testing</Description>
    <Company>TestCo</Company>
    <Product>LegacyApp</Product>
    <Copyright>Copyright © 2024</Copyright>
    <Version>1.0.0.0</Version>
  </PropertyGroup>

</Project>

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Remove legacy AssemblyInfo.cs and Properties dir

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Remove legacy AssemblyInfo.cs and Properties dir

data.reasoningText:
Now I have a clear picture. Let me identify all the legacy patterns and modernize the project.

Legacy patterns found:
1. `ToolsVersion="15.0"` and `xmlns` attribute — not needed in SDK-style
2. `<Import Project="$(MSBuildExtensionsPath)...Microsoft.Common.props" />` — replaced by SDK
3. Default `Configuration`/`Platform` properties — SDK defaults handle these
4. `ProjectGuid` — not needed in SDK-style
5. `FileAlignment` — SDK default
6. `AutoGenerateBindingRedirects` — SDK default for certain project types


7. `Deterministic` — SDK default (true)
8. Debug/Release `PropertyGroup` blocks — all are SDK defaults
9. `<Reference Include="System" />` etc. — implicit in .NET Framework SDK-style
10. Explicit `<Compile Include=...>` — SDK-style uses implicit globbing
11. `Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs` — SDK auto-generates these from project properties
12. `<Import Project="$(MSBuildToolsPath)\Microsoft.CSharp.targets" />` — replaced by SDK
13. `TargetFrameworkVersion` v4.7.2 → `TargetFramework` net472

Now I'll modernize the project file to be much leaner by switching to SDK-style format, removing the AssemblyInfo file since the SDK generates it automatically, and moving those assembly metadata values into the project properties instead.

    <Company>TestCo</Company>
    <Product>LegacyApp</Product>
    <Copyright>Copyright © 2024</Copyright>
    <Version>1.0.0.0</Version>
  </PropertyGroup>

</Project>

data.content:
**Done.** The project went from **47 lines → 14 lines**. Here's a summary of what changed:

- **Replaced** the entire legacy XML structure with `<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">`
- **Removed** manual `Import` of Common.props and CSharp.targets (SDK handles this)
- **Removed** `ProjectGuid`, `FileAlignment`, `Deterministic`, `AutoGenerateBindingRedirects`, `OutputType` (all SDK defaults; `Library` is the default OutputType)
- **Removed** Debug/Release `PropertyGroup` blocks (all values match SDK defaults)
- **Removed** explicit `<Compile Include>` items (SDK implicit globbing picks up all `.cs` files)
- **Removed** explicit `<Reference>` items for System assemblies (implicit for .NET Framework)
- **Deleted** `Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs` — metadata is now expressed as project properties (`Description`, `Company`, `Copyright`, `Version`), and the SDK auto-generates the assembly attributes
- **Converted** `TargetFrameworkVersion v4.7.2` → `TargetFramework net472`
